Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Bankruptcy Claims
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., and RASIER, LLC'S Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Bankruptcy Claims.

Defendants' unopposed motion "for stay of proceedings pending the outcome of bankruptcy claims" is granted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Jul-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, Llc?S Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiff?S Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Linda I. Yen
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
This matter is called with case nos. CGC-20-585158 (Line 8) and CGC-21-588761 (Line 11).

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200/wcubberly@whlaw.com, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert Atkins, Esq. of Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton Garrison LLP, 212.373.3000, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC;
Atty. Gary Wolenski, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121/gwolensky@buchalter.com, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC.

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Amended Complaint.

Matters called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. Because the basis for punitive damages is pled with insufficient particularity, that part of the motion is granted with leave to amend. Presumably plaintiff will be amending her complaint to plead fraud with greater particularity, and fraud plays a prominent role in punitive damages law. (See, e.g., CACI 3946.).

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
This matter is called with case nos. CGC-20-585158 (Line 8) and CGC-21-588761 (Line 11).

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200/wcubberly@whlaw.com, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert Atkins, Esq. of Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton Garrison LLP, 212.373.3000, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC;
Atty. Gary Wolenski, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121/gwolensky@buchalter.com, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC.

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matters called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrers to six of the first amended complaint's numbered causes of action are sustained or overruled as follows:

4 (fraud). Plaintiff fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

5-8 (assault, sexual battery, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress). Defendants' demurrer ignores the first amended complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier" and thus liable for assaults "regardless of whether such acts were committed within the course and scope of employment for Uber." (Cmpl., 17:18-26.) These allegations may or may not prove factually founded, but that is not for resolution at the pleading state. This demurrer is overruled.

9 (Ralph Act). The first amended complaint does not adequately plead the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to the demurring defendants, and Plaintiff fails to show on opposition that she can amend to do so. (Cmpl., 30:13-31:10; CACI 3063.) This demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
